methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Hippocratic AI, a Series B–stage AI & Software company founded by Munjal Shah that has raised $120M+, is valued through comparison to a peer set comprising five same-stage private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) spanning a valuation range of $500M to $32.0B, supplemented by one public comparable (Palantir).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $228M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M (floor) to $32.0B (ceiling). The peer set exhibits significant dispersion: the top quartile is anchored by Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, while the lower quartile includes Photoroom at $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low-end estimate of $194M, a median estimate of $2.6B, and a high-end estimate of $5.8B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around where Hippocratic AI sits within the peer distribution. Stage-progression anchors the conservative floor; peer-median suggests substantially higher valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, capital efficiency post-Series B, and whether the company's positioning aligns with lower-valuation peers or those commanding multibillion-dollar premiums.
